description: The rapid rise and collapse of Theranos, a blood-testing startup led by Elizabeth Holmes, centered on the fraudulent claim that their technology could perform hundreds of tests with a single drop of blood. Public sentiment shifted from admiration of a revolutionary female-led tech unicorn to widespread condemnation following the October 2015 Wall Street Journal exposé by John Carreyrou.

key_events.description: Theranos reaches peak valuation of $9 billion, widely praised in business media.
John Carreyrou publishes the Wall Street Journal exposé detailing systemic fraud.
CMS inspects Theranos labs and issues a report citing immediate jeopardy to patient health.
The SEC charges Elizabeth Holmes and Ramesh Balwani with massive fraud.
Theranos officially dissolves and ceases all operations.

calibration_notes: This scenario models the 'Hype-to-Fraud' trajectory, useful for calibrating how quickly public trust evaporates when structural corporate deception is exposed by whistleblowers and investigative journalism.
